wrapUp: The Round of 32 has delivered two penalty-shootout shocks as Paraguay eliminated Germany in Boston and Morocco knocked out the Netherlands in Monterrey, while Brazil needed Gabriel Martinelli's stoppage-time winner to see off Japan in Houston. Spain head into their last-32 tie against Austria on 2 July with a winger crisis after Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino suffered moderate injuries against Uruguay, and England must assess Reece James's hamstring before facing DR Congo in Atlanta on 1 July despite Harry Kane's record-breaking goals against Panama. The United States have arrived in the Bay Area ahead of their co-host knockout debut against Bosnia and Herzegovina at Levi's Stadium, while France, Mexico and Norway face high-stakes ties on 30 June. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 30 June 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Nico Williams faces 10-12 days out but Spain retain Round of 16 hope
stories.summary: Nico Williams suffered a moderate adductor muscle injury in added time against Uruguay on 26 June and is expected to miss Spain's Round of 32 meeting with Austria on 2 July in Los Angeles. The Athletic Bilbao winger criticised Nicolas de la Cruz's challenge as "completely unnecessary" on social media, while the Spanish federation said his recovery will determine whether he can return later in the tournament. Luis de la Fuente may lean on Lamine Yamal and Gavi out wide if Williams and the similarly injured Yeremy Pino are unavailable for the knockouts.

stories.headline: Tuchel confirms James will miss Panama clash with hamstring issue
stories.summary: Reece James did not travel to New Jersey for England's Group L finale against Panama on 27 June after picking up a minor hamstring problem in the goalless draw with Ghana. Thomas Tuchel said the Chelsea defender was on an accelerated rehabilitation programme and that hopes remained he could return for the Round of 32, though the manager acknowledged it "can be a tight one." James's absence compounds the loss of Tino Livramento from the squad, leaving Ezri Konsa, Djed Spence and Nico O'Reilly as cover at right-back before the DR Congo tie in Atlanta.

stories.headline: Pino avoids fracture but shoulder sprain clouds Spain availability
stories.summary: Yeremy Pino played on to the final whistle against Uruguay despite landing heavily on his left shoulder, with Luis de la Fuente initially fearing a broken collarbone. Scans on 27 June ruled out a fracture and diagnosed a moderate acromioclavicular sprain instead, though the Crystal Palace winger's day-to-day recovery will decide if he can feature before a potential Round of 16 tie. Spain already had doubts over Victor Munoz on the opposite flank, deepening de la Fuente's options problem ahead of the Austria fixture.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Kane breaks England World Cup scoring record in Panama win
stories.summary: Harry Kane headed Jude Bellingham's cross to become England's all-time leading World Cup goalscorer with 11 goals, surpassing Gary Lineker, as Thomas Tuchel's side beat Panama 2-0 on 27 June in New Jersey. Bellingham had opened the scoring from Bukayo Saka's corner, with Tuchel making five changes including first starts for Saka, Marcus Rashford and Morgan Rogers. The victory secured top spot in Group L and a Round of 32 date with DR Congo in Atlanta on 1 July.

stories.headline: Clarke resigns after Scotland fail to reach best-third cut
stories.summary: Steve Clarke resigned as Scotland manager after Croatia's 2-1 win over Ghana on 27 June confirmed the Tartan Army could not finish among the eight best third-placed teams. Scotland had ended Group C on three points with a minus-three goal difference after defeats to Morocco and Brazil, and Clarke had said he thought "we're going home" following the Miami hammering. The Scottish FA credited Clarke with leading the nation to its first men's World Cup in 28 years and back-to-back European Championship qualifications.

stories.headline: Tuchel rests Rice to manage minutes and suspension risk
stories.summary: Declan Rice was fit to face Panama on 27 June but Thomas Tuchel left him on the bench to manage a calf knock and avoid a second yellow card that would have ruled him out of the Round of 32. Jude Bellingham partnered Elliot Anderson in midfield while Morgan Rogers operated behind Harry Kane, with Rice expected to return against DR Congo once cards reset after the group stage. Tuchel said he wanted to gauge the intensity and refereeing of the Panama fixture before risking his vice-captain.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: USA base in South Bay ahead of Bosnia knockout tie
stories.summary: The United States men's national team arrived in the San Francisco Bay Area on 30 June to prepare for their Round of 32 fixture against Bosnia and Herzegovina at Levi's Stadium on 1 July. The co-hosts are expected to train with the San Jose Earthquakes as their base camp, with local authorities stepping up security and anti-trafficking measures modelled on February's Super Bowl. Demand for tickets has been strong for the first World Cup knockout match in the Bay Area, with fans already gathering ahead of the 17:00 local kick-off.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: France and Mexico headline final Round of 32 triple-header
stories.summary: The last three group-stage slots in the Round of 32 are decided on 30 June as Ivory Coast face Erling Haaland's Norway in Dallas, France meet Sweden at MetLife Stadium and co-host Mexico host Ecuador at the Estadio Azteca. France arrive as tournament favourites after winning all three group games, while Mexico have conceded just once in topping Group A and will seek a second World Cup knockout win 40 years after beating Bulgaria in 1986. The winners of France-Sweden and Paraguay's upset path converge in Philadelphia on 4 July.

stories.headline: Martinelli stoppage-time winner sends Brazil past Japan
stories.summary: Substitute Gabriel Martinelli scored in the fifth minute of added time as Brazil came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 on 29 June in Houston, denying Hajime Moriyasu's side a first-ever World Cup knockout victory. Kaishu Sano had put Japan ahead before Casemiro equalised with a header from Gabriel Magalhaes's cross, and Bruno Guimaraes slipped in Martinelli for the decisive finish. Brazil will face the winner of Ivory Coast's meeting with Norway in the Round of 16 on 5 July in New Jersey.

stories.headline: Paraguay eliminate Germany on penalties in Boston shock
stories.summary: Paraguay knocked four-time champions Germany out of the World Cup on 29 June, winning 4-3 on penalties after a 1-1 draw at Boston Stadium in Germany's first knockout defeat via a shootout. Jose Canale converted the decisive spot-kick after Jonathan Tah blazed over, capping a defiant display in which Paraguay completed just 161 passes to Germany's 719. Julian Nagelsmann's side failed to keep a clean sheet in any of their four matches, and Paraguay now face the winner of France's tie with Sweden in Philadelphia on 4 July.

stories.headline: Saibari sends Morocco past Netherlands in Monterrey shootout
stories.summary: Morocco beat the Netherlands 3-2 on penalties on 29 June after Issa Diop's stoppage-time header forced extra time in a 1-1 draw at Estadio Monterrey. Yassine Bounou saved Crysencio Summerville's fourth kick before Ismael Saibari sealed the win, sending Walid Regragui's side into a last-16 meeting with Canada in Houston on 4 July. The result marked the earliest World Cup exit for the Dutch since 1990 despite Cody Gakpo giving Ronald Koeman's team the lead in the second half.
